test_suite.details: 3309 passed, 0 failed. All internal anchors, showTab targets, wins.json schema, HTML consistency, data cross-refs, sections.json schema, prose content, and prediction panels verified.

internal_anchors.details: Test suite Section 3 (Internal Links) verified all href='#...' targets and showTab() calls. No manual re-check needed.

tab_structure.nav_chain_valid: sectionNav not used (tab navigation uses tab-bar buttons)
tab_structure.details: 11 tabs found, all with unique IDs. All 12 unique showTab targets resolve (including 'overview' which maps to id='overview'). id='overview' confirmed present in HTML.

external_links.notes: gracefo.jpl.nasa.gov returned 403 (likely bot-blocking, not a true broken link). All other spot-checked URLs resolved successfully.
external_links.details: Spot-checked 10 key data source URLs. All reachable. 2 minor redirects (www prefix changes). DOI/publisher links skipped per policy.

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json has 70 entries (IDs 001-069 plus 058b). HTML scorecard correctly shows 69 (the dome's claimed count). Verdict breakdown in scorecard matches data: Refuted by Data=9, Self-Contradicted=14, Misleading=22, Std Model Explains=17, Not Demonstrated=4, Unfalsifiable=4. new_in_v51=31. Acknowledged failures=8 (matches uncounted-failures.json entries). Build produces consistent counts.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 70 WINs have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields. Detail group cross-references are consistent (8 groups verified). WIN panel IDs use 'winNNN' format in HTML (e.g., id='win001'). Test suite Section 5 (Data Cross-References) validated all WIN detail blocks.

progressive_disclosure.details: All WINs have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict. Test suite verified prose content integrity and prediction panels (77 panels: 12 tombstone, 65 mined, 10 operational).

discoverability.llms_txt: exists, contains review URL and dome model reference
discoverability.sitemap_xml: exists, valid urlset, contains main URL
discoverability.robots_txt: exists, has Allow:/ and Sitemap directive
discoverability.uncounted_failures: 8 entries, all valid FAIL-NNN format, no duplicates, all required fields present
discoverability.meta_tags: description, og:title, og:description, twitter:card, ClaimReview ld+json all present
discoverability.details: All discoverability infrastructure files present and well-formed.

expansion_tracker.orphaned_files: 109 files in expansions/ without tracker entries (mostly PRED-assessment-*, ISS-*, MODE5-audit files — these are analyst outputs that use the expansions directory but are not tracked as EXP items)
expansion_tracker.details: next_id is safe. 1 ID gap (EXP-052). 2 stale pending items >48h. Large number of orphaned files but these are non-EXP output files (prediction assessments, issue research) that legitimately live in the expansions directory.

priority_queue_coupling.details: EXP-050 (category-proposal-writeup, status=revision-submitted) has no matching entry in the curmudgeon priority queue. This means the prose revision will bypass adversarial review.

workspace_sync_skips.details: No direction violations in last 24h. 6 mtime-guard skips (normal): category-proposals/CAT-005.json (3x), human-notes.json (3x).

digest_coverage.details: 14 curmudgeon reviews never processed by decider. Mix of prefixes: 10 SEC-*, 1 HOLISTIC-*, 1 PRED-*, 1 WIN-*, 1 part1-*. This exceeds the >10 threshold for a systematic gap. The digest pipeline fix from 2026-04-12 may not have fully caught up, or new reviews arrived faster than processing.

build_reproducibility.details: CRITICAL: Published docs/index.html (committed at V4.9.4) differs from freshly built output by 3006 insertions and 646 deletions. Major missing features in the committed version: meta tags (description, og:*, twitter:*, ClaimReview ld+json), progressive disclosure CSS, code analysis tags, prediction panel styling, scorecard enhancements, and substantial prose/content updates. The published site is significantly behind the current source data and templates. Last commit to docs/index.html was V4.9.4; multiple data and template changes have occurred since without a publish cycle.

project_documentation.details: CLAUDE.md exists with schedule table, file ownership rules, and command-based count queries. No hardcoded counts detected in CLAUDE.md (uses node -e commands throughout). Key file paths spot-checked: data/wins.json, data/sections.json, build.js, monitor/prompts/*.md all exist.

issues_found.description: Published docs/index.html is 3654 diff-lines behind the current build output. The committed HTML (V4.9.4) is missing meta tags, progressive disclosure, code analysis tags, prediction panels, and extensive prose updates.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Run 'node build.js publish' from the clean clone to rebuild and push. This is the single most important action — the published site does not reflect current data.

issues_found.description: 14 curmudgeon reviews are unprocessed (>10 threshold). Breakdown: SEC-* (10), HOLISTIC-* (1), PRED-* (1), WIN-* (1), part1-* (1). These findings are invisible to the decider.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Verify digest-reviews.js processes all review prefixes. Run decider to catch up on backlog. The 2026-04-12 filter fix may need a forced re-digest of older reviews.

issues_found.description: EXP-050 (category-proposal-writeup, status=revision-submitted) is not represented in the curmudgeon priority queue. Prose revision will bypass adversarial review.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider should push EXP-050 onto priority-queue.json with target_id='EXP-050'. Integrity agent does not self-fix — decider reconciles.

issues_found.description: 2 expansion items have been pending >48h: EXP-062 (since 2026-04-10) and EXP-064 (since 2026-04-11). The analyst may have lost track.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider should check if these are blocked or abandoned and either reassign or close them.

issues_found.description: ID gap: EXP-052 is missing from the tracker. May indicate a lost allocation or write collision.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Investigate whether EXP-052 was allocated and dropped. Gap is minor but should be logged.

issues_found.description: 2 external links have minor redirect changes: ncei.noaa.gov -> www.ncei.noaa.gov, www.intermagnet.org -> intermagnet.org (www prefix changes). gracefo.jpl.nasa.gov returns 403 (likely bot-blocking).
issues_found.suggested_fix: Update URLs to use canonical (post-redirect) forms to avoid unnecessary 301s. The 403 on gracefo.jpl.nasa.gov is likely a false positive.

summary: OVERALL FAIL. The most critical issue is massive build drift: the published HTML is 3654 diff-lines behind the current build, missing meta tags, progressive disclosure, prediction panels, and extensive content updates — the live site does not match the source data. Additionally, 14 curmudgeon reviews remain unprocessed by the decider (exceeding the 10-item threshold), and EXP-050 (a category-proposal-writeup) is missing from the curmudgeon priority queue, meaning its prose revision bypasses adversarial review. The test suite passes cleanly (3309/3309), all discoverability infrastructure is intact, the expansion tracker's next_id is safe, and no workspace-sync direction violations were found. Two expansion items are stale-pending (>48h). Immediate action needed: run 'node build.js publish' to close the build gap, then address the digest backlog and priority-queue decoupling.
